summary: The Australian Government has awarded $12.7 million through stage 2 of the Critical Technologies Challenge Program to support eight quantum technology projects. Notable recipients include Loughan Technology Group, receiving $2.4 million to create Australia’s first real-time mine-site sensor for rare-earth elements; La Trobe University, receiving $1.1 million for developing a hybrid quantum–classical optimisation system to enhance energy efficiency in data centre cooling; and Miniprobes, awarded $1.2 million to advance its quantum optical imaging for rapid diabetes assessment. The program, which offers up to $36 million in total, aims to demonstrate quantum technology solutions addressing nationally significant challenges and is part of Australia’s National Quantum Strategy.
